de.uka.ilkd.key.informationflow.macros

Class UseInformationFlowContractMacro

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.macros.AbstractProofMacro
      • de.uka.ilkd.key.macros.StrategyProofMacro
        • de.uka.ilkd.key.informationflow.macros.UseInformationFlowContractMacro
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    ProofMacro

    public class UseInformationFlowContractMacro
extends StrategyProofMacro
    The macro UseInformationFlowContractMacro applies all applicable information flow contracts.

    The rules that are applied can be set in ADMITTED_RULENAMES.

    Author:
    christoph

    • Nested Class Summary

      Nested Classes 
      Modifier and Type Class and Description
      protected class  UseInformationFlowContractMacro.PropExpansionStrategy
      This strategy accepts all rule apps for which the rule name starts with a string in the admitted set and rejects everything else.

      • Nested classes/interfaces inherited from interface de.uka.ilkd.key.macros.ProofMacro

        ProofMacro.ProgressBarListener

    • Method Detail

      • getName

        public String getName()
        Description copied from interface: ProofMacro
        Gets the name of this macro. Used as menu entry
        Returns:
        a non-null constant string

      • getCategory

        public String getCategory()
        Description copied from interface: ProofMacro
        Gets the category of this macro. Used as name of the menu under which the macro is sorted. Return null if no submenu is to be created.
        Returns:
        a constant string, or null

      • getDescription

        public String getDescription()
        Description copied from interface: ProofMacro
        Gets the description of this macro. Used as tooltip.
        Returns:
        a non-null constant string

      • ruleApplicationInContextAllowed

        protected boolean ruleApplicationInContextAllowed(RuleApp ruleApp,
                                      PosInOccurrence pio,
                                      Goal goal)
        Checks whether the application of the passed rule is ok in the given context.

        Parameters:
        ruleApp - rule to be applied
        pio - context
        goal - context
        Returns:
        true if rule may be applied
